title: ProtocolLab gRPC over HTTP/2 Contract Verification
scope: Verify public gRPC/H2 contract structure only; do not verify a gRPC runtime, client, server, executor, or benchmark.
verification_method: Schema validation, deterministic message-contract inspection, suite and profile reference resolution, fixture checks, and standards-link inspection.
procedure: Validate the v2 service descriptor and confirm all nine canonical methods occur exactly once with the required streaming flags and terminal behavior.
Validate unary, server-streaming, client-streaming, and bidirectional-streaming success scenarios.

Confirm every gRPC scenario declares application/grpc+proto as its canonical request and response representation and exactly application/grpc plus application/grpc+proto as its accepted protobuf response set; reject missing-base, JSON-subtype, and gRPC-Web substitutions.
Confirm trailers-only INVALID_ARGUMENT, deadline-exceeded, and client-cancellation scenarios declare distinct expected terminal outcomes and disable retries and hedging.
Confirm gzip validation is decompression-based and fixed ASCII/binary metadata resolves to the canonical profile.
Recompute payload, protobuf, and uncompressed gRPC-envelope hashes for empty, 128-byte, 1 KiB, and 1 MiB message fixtures.
Confirm pre-established and new-channel lifecycles use separate scenario identities, profiles, and measured windows.
Confirm comparison profiles remain non-publishable and gRPC-Web/H3 remain excluded.
expected_result: The gRPC/H2 family has bounded success, terminal-outcome, compression, metadata, size, and lifecycle contracts without implying executable support or extending to gRPC-Web/H3.
